programmes.show_description: Stranger in the House: Detective drama series. DI Frost exposes a junior officer to danger in his effort to trap a particularly dangerous rapist that is at large on his patch.
Going Steady: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan's attempts to woo the inspector's niece at a dinner party end in disaster.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Charades: Detective drama series. Wycliffe and his team move in to investigate the death of a man found blasted by a shotgun at a Cornish holiday home.
Lost Contact: A body with gunshot wounds to the head is washed ashore. Although the corpse is unidentifiable, two women each claim that it must be their missing husband.
The Dead of Winter: When a body is found on an Oxford tour bus, Lewis and Hathaway are led to a sprawling Oxford estate where Hathaway spent much of his childhood.
Deguello: The murder of an Oxford librarian and a local tragedy test Endeavour and the team to their limits, as they uncover the truth behind the crime that has haunted them all.
City Slickers: Comedy-drama series. Louisa deals with some new and very annoying neighbours, Mrs Tishell visits the Doc with a rash, and Joan and Bert have their cars keyed.
The Admirer: Comedy-drama series. Louisa is angry to discover that she has a rival for Martin's affections, and Joan finds that she has an admirer who is 30 years younger than her.
Orpheus in the Undergrowth: Light-hearted drama series. Laura is planning to construct a memorial garden near her daughter's home when the body of an alcoholic is found at the site.
They Understand Me in Paris: Drama series about two gardeners who are amateur sleuths. The duo are restoring the gardens of a French villa when their host's husband is found dead.
Detective drama series. When a body is found on a bus during a morning rush hour in Bath, the duo are sucked into a mystery involving a precious artefact.
Endless Night: While staying with a recently widowed friend, Miss Marple meets charming drifter Mike Rogers. Events then take a sinister turn when a dangerous killer strikes...
A Grain of Truth: The world of organic baking begins to crumble after a series of incidents at the newly-revamped Larkton's Mill.
Blind Spot: DCI Vera Stanhope investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist found dead on a Northumberland landfill site.
Drama based on real events. When cracks start to appear in Jane and Tommy's whirlwind relationship, a make-or-break holiday in the south of France leads to disaster.
The conclusion of the drama based on real events. Jane's fairytale falls apart as she faces a shocking murder trial - losing her grip on reality, a jury must decide her fate.
The Squeeze: Enduring '70s sitcom. Stan and Jack try to convince the inspector to buy an old motorbike belonging to Arthur, but the test drive does not go quite as they planned.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
A New Lease of Life: Classic British sitcom. Simon begins to feel as though his life is passing far too quickly for his own good, so he decides to make a big change to his life.
The 'L' Bus: Enduring '70s sitcom. When Stan needs to transport a new bed for Arthur and Olive to the house, he uses a passenger-free bus that normally employed for training drivers.
Viv confronts a witness found by her private investigator and Bob begins to doubt his wife. The Dingles steal the pub's furniture as Val prepares for the competition.
An anxious Tina prepares for her abortion, racked with guilt about keeping David in the dark. Tony makes Liam an offer and Norris is blamed for informing on Kirk.
Gail is forced to tell David about Tina's abortion, and in a fit of rage he pushes his mother down the stairs. Nick arrives in the pub to see Ryan, and Kirk visits Chesney.
Deep Waters: Feature-length police drama. When a beautiful student is attacked on a campus, Frost has to decide whether it is an isolated incident or the start of a nightmare.
Strike Up the Band: The police investigate when the rivalry between two brass bands leads to violence, and Dawn finds romance with a handsome stranger but her happiness may not last.
Return Crossing: A widow's life is endangered by the dodgy business dealings of her late husband, and the locals help a visitor on his romantic quest.
Market for Murder: When the leader of a reading club is found battered to death, the club's activities are exposed and long-held secrets and desires return to haunt its members.
Gray visits Perdy, who agrees to drop the custody petition in return for access to the baby. Ashley asks the Dolands about Mel's whereabouts, and Val and Pollard plan a wedding.
Gray tells Katie that he has painted the nursery. Melanie notices Ashley walks past Tenant's Cottage for the third time and tells Greg that they are under surveillance.
As Gail lies motionless at the bottom of the stairs, a terrified David runs out of the back door and makes his way to Tina's. Tony quizzes Carla about her relationship with Liam.
As Gail lies unconscious in intensive care, the doctors tell a panic-stricken David his mother is badly injured but should come round soon. Meanwhile, the police suspect foul play.
The Hospital Job: A search for stolen antiques becomes life-threatening for Mason and Wetherby, and prejudices are stirred by the arrival of a group of Taoist monks.
Ups and Downs: A cat burglar is on the prowl, targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Carol catches the eye of a dashing visitor while Peggy plays matchmaker for David.
Dressed to Kill: When drag queens sashay into the village of Elverton-cum-Latterly, they are ostracised when their charity event clashes with an annual dominoes competition.
Scherzo: Crime drama. It's the summer of 1971, and Endeavour and the team are called to investigate the murder of a cab driver found dead in his taxi on the outskirts of Oxford.
